preview.summary: A pivotal Group G meeting as New Zealand's All Whites, the clear underdogs, face an Egypt side eyeing a first-ever knockout run. Egypt's Salah-led attack should dominate the ball, with New Zealand banking on physicality and set-pieces around Chris Wood.
preview.homeForm: New Zealand return to the World Cup ranked 85th, the lowest seed in Group G, captained by record scorer Chris Wood.
preview.awayForm: Egypt qualified as African heavyweights ranked 29th, with Mohamed Salah their top scorer in qualifying with nine goals.
preview.homeKey: Chris Wood, Nottingham Forest forward and all-time top scorer.
preview.awayKey: Mohamed Salah, captain and talisman chasing Egypt's scoring record.
storylines: Egypt hunt a first-ever World Cup quarter-final
Wood and Smith become first Kiwi men at two World Cups
Marmoush and Salah against a resolute New Zealand block
preview.h2h: Their only previous meeting, in 2024, ended in a 1-0 Egypt win.
